title: onosma helvetica subsp. vaudensis
intro: Onosma helvetica subsp. vaudensis, also known as Vaud goldenrod, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive golden color. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its iridoid glycoside content, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Onosma helvetica subsp. vaudensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a promising herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and flowers to create teas, tinctures, or capsules for medicinal use.
